_notes: Priority queue for the SBU AI cluster (defq): h100 = 4x H100 80GB; quadro1-2 = 8x RTX 8000 48GB; tesla1-2 = 8x V100 32GB; orion = 4x V100 + 1.5TB RAM.
est_weights_gb is the approximate q4 download/VRAM footprint of the default Ollama quantization; add ~15-25% for KV-cache/overhead when choosing GPUs.

tag_checked=false means the tag was read off ollama.com/library on 2026-07-08 but not yet pulled here - verify with 'ollama pull' (a wrong tag fails immediately with a 404-style error, nothing is wasted).
nodelist/gres/mem/time override queue_defaults when run_queue.sh submits the job. mem is deliberately generous because Ollama mmaps weights and Slurm's memory cgroup counts that page cache.
One job per model, chained one-after-one with --dependency by slurm/run_queue.sh.

models.why: Fast, reasoning-capable, fits any GPU on the cluster. Ideal first real run to shake down the pipeline.
Newest Google open model (library lists 12b/26b/31b). 26b is the quality/speed sweet spot; 31b also fits a single RTX 8000 if you want the top size.
Reasoning model - interesting to see if chain-of-thought helps score prediction. Full R1/V4 don't fit the cluster; this distill does.
Meta's 70B flagship (3.3 supersedes 3.1-70b at the same size). Needs one H100 80GB, or use --gres=gpu:2 on quadro instead.
The big gpt-oss - direct 20b vs 120b scaling comparison within one family. One H100 80GB.
GLM family representative that actually fits (30B class). GLM-5/5.1 are 744B MoE - see not_feasible.
Mistral representative (24B). Mistral Large 3 is a big MoE; check its size on the library before attempting.
Strong small model; runs anywhere including V100 nodes.
Small baseline - anchors the leaderboard's low end and directly answers 'does scale matter here?'
Tiny baseline; nearly free to run.
Optional extra: strong recent open family, keeps the benchmark honest. Fits RTX 8000/H100 (not V100 32GB).
Stretch goal: ~230B-class MoE, needs 2x H100 (or try 4x RTX 8000 with --nodelist=quadro1 --gres=gpu:4). Run last so a failure costs nothing.

not_feasible.reason: 744B-total MoE, ~400GB+ quantized - exceeds any node's total VRAM (max is 384GB on quadro nodes, minus overhead). A CPU-offload experiment on orion (1.5TB RAM) is possible but expect minutes per token; user asked for glm-5.2 which does not appear on ollama.com/library as of 2026-07-08 - latest listed is glm-5.1.
Frontier-size MoE, same VRAM problem as GLM-5.
~230GB q4 dense (not MoE) - would be brutally slow even if sharded across 8x RTX 8000.
